title: tithymalus dilatatus
intro: Tithymalus dilatatus, also known as the Euphorbia peplus or radium weed, is a toxic plant species with a milky sap and a leafy, green appearance. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce vomiting, purging, and skin conditions treatment due to its toxic compound, ingenol mebutate. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as treating warts, skin cancer, and other skin conditions, as well as being used as an anti-inflammatory and antibacterial agent. Tithymalus dilatatus is typically used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and ointments, which are applied topically to affected areas.
